root.description: The attribute to use as the hash (partition) key. Must also be defined as an attribute
root.name: hash_key

root.description: Whether or not to enable encryption at rest using an AWS managed KMS customer master key (CMK)
root.name: server_side_encryption_enabled

root.description: A map of read autoscaling settings. `max_capacity` is the only required key. See example in examples/autoscaling
root.name: autoscaling_read

root.description: Name of the table to restore. Must match the name of an existing table.
root.name: restore_source_name

root.description: Controls if DynamoDB table and associated resources are created
root.name: create_table

root.description: Updated Terraform resource management timeouts
root.name: timeouts

root.description: Whether or not to enable autoscaling. See note in README about this setting
root.name: autoscaling_enabled

root.description: A map of write autoscaling settings. `max_capacity` is the only required key. See example in examples/autoscaling
root.name: autoscaling_write

root.description: Whether to ignore changes lifecycle to global secondary indices, useful for provisioned tables with scaling
root.name: ignore_changes_global_secondary_index

root.description: Time of the point-in-time recovery point to restore.
root.name: restore_date_time

root.description: The number of read units for this table. If the billing_mode is PROVISIONED, this field should be greater than 0
root.name: read_capacity

root.description: Whether to enable point-in-time recovery
root.name: point_in_time_recovery_enabled

root.description: Indicates whether ttl is enabled
root.name: ttl_enabled

root.description: The ARN of the CMK that should be used for the AWS KMS encryption. This attribute should only be specified if the key is different from the default DynamoDB CMK, alias/aws/dynamodb.
root.name: server_side_encryption_kms_key_arn

root.description: Configurations for importing s3 data into a new table.
root.name: import_table

root.description: Sets the number of warm read and write units for the specified table
root.name: warm_throughput

root.description: The JSON definition of the resource-based policy.
root.name: resource_policy

root.description: Indicates whether Streams are to be enabled (true) or disabled (false).
root.name: stream_enabled

root.description: A map of default autoscaling settings
root.name: autoscaling_defaults

root.description: A map of index autoscaling configurations. See example in examples/autoscaling
root.name: autoscaling_indexes

root.description: The storage class of the table. Valid values are STANDARD and STANDARD_INFREQUENT_ACCESS
root.name: table_class

root.description: Enables deletion protection for table
root.name: deletion_protection_enabled

root.description: Region where this resource will be managed. Defaults to the Region set in the provider configuration
root.name: region

root.description: Name of the DynamoDB table
root.name: name

root.description: Controls how you are billed for read/write throughput and how you manage capacity. The valid values are PROVISIONED or PAY_PER_REQUEST
root.name: billing_mode

root.description: A map of tags to add to all resources
root.name: tags

root.description: If set, restores table to the most recent point-in-time recovery point.
root.name: restore_to_latest_time

root.description: The number of write units for this table. If the billing_mode is PROVISIONED, this field should be greater than 0
root.name: write_capacity

root.description: Region names for creating replicas for a global DynamoDB table.
root.name: replica_regions

root.description: When an item in the table is modified, StreamViewType determines what information is written to the table's stream. Valid values are KEYS_ONLY, NEW_IMAGE, OLD_IMAGE, NEW_AND_OLD_IMAGES.
root.name: stream_view_type

root.description: Describe an LSI on the table; these can only be allocated at creation so you cannot change this definition after you have created the resource.
root.name: local_secondary_indexes

root.description: The attribute to use as the range (sort) key. Must also be defined as an attribute
root.name: range_key

root.description: Number of preceding days for which continuous backups are taken and maintained. Default 35
root.name: point_in_time_recovery_period_in_days

root.description: Sets the maximum number of read and write units for the specified on-demand table
root.name: on_demand_throughput

root.description: ARN of the source table to restore. Must be supplied for cross-region restores.
root.name: restore_source_table_arn

root.description: Witness Region in a Multi-Region Strong Consistency deployment. Note This must be used alongside a single replica with consistency_mode set to STRONG. Other combinations will fail to provision
root.name: global_table_witness

root.description: List of nested attribute definitions. Only required for hash_key and range_key attributes. Each attribute has two properties: name - (Required) The name of the attribute, type - (Required) Attribute type, which must be a scalar type: S, N, or B for (S)tring, (N)umber or (B)inary data
root.name: attributes

root.description: The name of the table attribute to store the TTL timestamp in
root.name: ttl_attribute_name

root.description: Describe a GSI for the table; subject to the normal limits on the number of GSIs, projected attributes, etc.
root.name: global_secondary_indexes

root.description: ID of the DynamoDB table
root.name: dynamodb_table_id

root.description: The ARN of the Table Stream
root.name: dynamodb_table_stream_arn

root.description: A timestamp, in ISO 8601 format of the Table Stream
root.name: dynamodb_table_stream_label

root.description: Map of Table replicas by region
root.name: dynamodb_table_replicas

root.description: Map of the Table replicas ARNs
root.name: dynamodb_table_replica_arns

root.description: Map of the Table replicas stream ARNs
root.name: dynamodb_table_replica_stream_arns

root.description: Map of the timestamps of the Table replicas stream
root.name: dynamodb_table_replica_stream_labels

root.description: ARN of the DynamoDB table
root.name: dynamodb_table_arn

examples:
global_secondary_indexes = [
    {
      name               = "TitleIndex"
      hash_key           = "title"
      range_key          = "age"
      projection_type    = "INCLUDE"
      non_key_attributes = ["id"]
      write_capacity     = 10
      read_capacity      = 10
    }
  ]
